content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 13, 2017


Roxanne M. Diaz, Esq.
Norwalk City Attorney
Richards Watson Gershon
355 South Grand Avenue, 40th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90071-3101

Re:	Your Request for Advice
	Our File No. W- 17-171

Dear Ms. Diaz:

	This letter is to notify you that we have withdrawn your request for advice on behalf of Vice Mayor Leonard Shryock and Bing Hyun, Economic Development Manager for the City of Norwalk regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act and Government Code Section 1090.

	On September 7, 2017, we requested additional information from you on behalf of the requestors. We have not received a response to date. As provided in an email on October 19, 2017, your request would be considered withdrawn if we did not receive a response by October 25, 2017. Please contact our office if, at a later date, you wish to renew this request.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Assistant General Counsel

By:	L. Karen Harrison
	Counsel, Legal Division

LKH:jgl

sections.question_synthetic: Vice Mayor Leonard Shryock and Bing Hyun (Economic Development Manager) requested FPPC advice regarding conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act and Government Code Section 1090.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because the requestors failed to provide requested additional information by the October 25, 2017 deadline set after an initial request on September 7, 2017.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no traditional Q/C sections. Document contains clear explanation of withdrawal grounds (non-response to information request within deadline). File number W-17-171 indicates withdrawal status.

QUESTION: Vice Mayor Leonard Shryock and Bing Hyun (Economic Development Manager) requested FPPC advice regarding conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act and Government Code Section 1090.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because the requestors failed to provide requested additional information by the October 25, 2017 deadline set after an initial request on September 7, 2017.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter notifying the requestor (Norwalk City Attorney) that their advice request on behalf of Vice Mayor Leonard Shryock and Bing Hyun regarding conflict of interest provisions has been withdrawn due to failure to provide requested additional information by the specified deadline.
